title: Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup

seo.title: How to Make Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup — Easy Mexican Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup. This easy Mexican recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

easy Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup recipe
authentic Mexican Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Mexican Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup — a beloved Mexican classic

description: Pozole Rojo is a hearty and flavorful Mexican soup, beloved for its rich red broth and tender hominy. This traditional dish, often served for celebrations, features succulent pork or chicken simmered with a blend of dried chiles, creating a deep, complex flavor that warms the soul. Garnished with fresh toppings, it's a truly satisfying culinary experience.

ingredients.notes: half quartered, half finely chopped for garnish

instructions.title: Cook the Pork
instructions.text: Place the pork shoulder cubes in a large pot or Dutch oven. Add half of the quartered onion, 3 garlic cloves, and cover with enough water or broth to fully submerge the pork. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until the pork is very tender and easily shreds. Skim off any foam that rises to the top during the first 30 minutes.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chile Sauce
instructions.text: While the pork cooks, rehydrate the dried chiles. Place the stemmed and seeded guajillo and ancho chiles in a bowl and cover with hot water. Let them soak for about 20-30 minutes until soft. Drain the chiles, reserving a little of the soaking liquid. In a blender, combine the rehydrated chiles, remaining 3 garlic cloves, and the other half of the quartered onion. Add about 1/2 cup of fresh water or reserved chile soaking liquid. Blend until very smooth, adding more liquid if needed to achieve a pourable consistency. Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl to remove any solids or skin bits.

instructions.title: Shred the Pork and Combine Ingredients
instructions.text: Once the pork is tender, remove it from the pot and set aside to cool slightly. Reserve the cooking liquid. Once cool enough to handle, shred the pork using two forks. Discard the cooked onion and garlic from the pot. Return the shredded pork to the pot. Add the strained chile sauce, drained hominy, dried oregano, ground cumin, and the reserved pork cooking liquid (or fresh broth/water to make 8 cups total if needed).

instructions.title: Simmer the Pozole
instructions.text: Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat to low. Cover and let the pozole simmer for at least 30 minutes, or up to an hour, allowing the flavors to meld. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. This simmering time is crucial for developing the rich taste.

instructions.title: Adjust Seasoning
instructions.text: Taste the pozole and season generously with salt and black pepper as needed. The flavors should be robust and well-balanced. If the broth is too thick, you can add a little more water or broth until it reaches your desired consistency.

instructions.title: Serve with Garnishes
instructions.text: Ladle the hot pozole into individual bowls. Serve immediately with a generous array of garnishes on the side, allowing each person to customize their bowl. Essential garnishes include fresh lime wedges, finely chopped white onion, thinly sliced radishes, and shredded cabbage. Diced avocado and crispy tostadas or tortilla chips are also excellent additions.

tips: For a deeper flavor, lightly toast the dried chiles in a dry pan for 30-60 seconds per side before rehydrating. Be careful not to burn them, as this will make them bitter.
The quality of your hominy makes a difference. While canned is convenient, if you can find dried hominy and cook it from scratch, the texture and flavor will be even better.
Pozole tastes even better the next day! Make a large batch and store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop.
article: Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup is a beloved Mexican Soups and Stews dish enjoyed across Mexican. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
